desc.en: Describe people, things, places, times, and reasons with precise connected sentences.
desc.zh: 用准确连贯的句子描述人物、事物、地点、时间和原因。
desc.es: Describe personas, cosas, lugares, momentos y razones con oraciones precisas y conectadas.

desc.en: Build reliable intuition for place, time, movement, and common preposition patterns.
desc.zh: 建立对地点、时间、移动和常见介词搭配的可靠语感。
desc.es: Desarrolla intuición para lugar, tiempo, movimiento y combinaciones comunes.

desc.en: Choose verb forms by timeline, duration, completion, and present relevance.
desc.zh: 根据时间线、持续性、完成性和当前关联选择动词形式。
desc.es: Elige formas verbales según tiempo, duración, finalización y relevancia presente.

desc.en: Express facts, possibilities, imagined situations, regrets, and conditions.
desc.zh: 表达事实、可能性、假设情境、遗憾和条件。
desc.es: Expresa hechos, posibilidades, situaciones imaginarias, arrepentimientos y condiciones.

desc.en: Shift focus between the doer, the action, and the result across timelines.
desc.zh: 在施事者、动作和结果之间灵活转换焦点。
desc.es: Cambia el foco entre quien actúa, la acción y el resultado en distintos tiempos.

desc.en: Retell statements, questions, requests, and ideas accurately and naturally.
desc.zh: 准确自然地转述陈述、问题、请求和观点。
desc.es: Cuenta declaraciones, preguntas, peticiones e ideas con precisión y naturalidad.

desc.en: Express ability, permission, advice, obligation, probability, and deduction.
desc.zh: 表达能力、许可、建议、义务、可能性和推断。
desc.es: Expresa capacidad, permiso, consejo, obligación, probabilidad y deducción.

desc.en: Signal whether nouns are known, specific, general, countable, or quantified.
desc.zh: 说明名词是否已知、特指、泛指、可数或带数量。
desc.es: Indica si los sustantivos son conocidos, específicos, generales, contables o cuantificados.

desc.en: Connect ideas by addition, contrast, cause, result, condition, and concession.
desc.zh: 用并列、转折、原因、结果、条件和让步连接观点。
desc.es: Conecta ideas mediante adición, contraste, causa, resultado, condición y concesión.

desc.en: Control gerunds, infinitives, causatives, and meaning-changing verb patterns.
desc.zh: 掌握动名词、不定式、使役结构和会改变意义的动词搭配。
desc.es: Domina gerundios, infinitivos, causativos y patrones que cambian el significado.

desc.en: Build, question, negate, combine, and repair complete English sentences.
desc.zh: 构建、提问、否定、组合并修复完整的英语句子。
desc.es: Construye, pregunta, niega, combina y corrige oraciones completas en inglés.

desc.en: Use emphasis, inversion, register, compression, and idiomatic grammar in context.
desc.zh: 在语境中使用强调、倒装、语体、压缩结构和地道语法。
desc.es: Usa énfasis, inversión, registro, reducción y gramática idiomática en contexto.

d.en: Identify the relative clause and the noun it describes.
d.zh: 识别定语从句以及它所修饰的名词。
d.es: Identifica la cláusula relativa y el sustantivo que describe.

q.en:
Which part of this sentence is the relative clause?
The woman who called me is my manager.
q.zh:
下面哪个部分是定语从句？
The woman who called me is my manager.
q.es:
¿Qué parte es la cláusula relativa?
The woman who called me is my manager.

t.en: A relative clause starts with a relative pronoun (who/which/that/where/when) and describes the noun before it.
t.zh: 定语从句由关系代词（who/which/that等）引导，修饰它前面的名词。
t.es: Una cláusula relativa empieza con un pronombre relativo y describe al sustantivo anterior.

en: "The woman" is the antecedent — the noun being described by the relative clause.
✓ Correct! "who called me" is the relative clause. It begins with the relative pronoun "who" and describes "the woman."
"is my manager" is the main clause predicate, not the relative clause.
"my manager" is the complement of the main clause, not part of the relative clause.
zh: "The woman" 是被修饰的名词（先行词），不是定语从句本身。
✓ 正确！"who called me" 是定语从句，由关系代词 who 引导，修饰 the woman。
"is my manager" 是主句的谓语部分，不属于定语从句。
"my manager" 是主句的表语，不是定语从句的一部分。
es: "The woman" es el antecedente — el sustantivo descrito por la cláusula relativa.
✓ ¡Correcto! "who called me" es la cláusula relativa. Empieza con el pronombre relativo "who" y describe a "the woman."
"is my manager" es el predicado de la oración principal, no la cláusula relativa.
"my manager" es el complemento de la oración principal, no parte de la cláusula relativa.

q.en:
Which part is the relative clause?
I read the book that you recommended.
q.zh:
下面哪个部分是定语从句？
I read the book that you recommended.
q.es:
¿Qué parte es la cláusula relativa?
I read the book that you recommended.

t.en: The relative clause comes AFTER the noun it describes. Look for who/which/that + a verb.
t.zh: 定语从句紧跟在被修饰的名词后面，找 who/which/that + 动词的组合。
t.es: La cláusula relativa va DESPUÉS del sustantivo que describe. Busca who/which/that + verbo.

en: "I read" is the main clause subject and verb.
"the book" is the antecedent — the noun the relative clause describes.
✓ Correct! "that you recommended" is the relative clause describing "the book."
"the book that" mixes the antecedent with the start of the clause — it's not a single unit.
zh: "I read" 是主句的主语和谓语，不是定语从句。
"the book" 是被修饰的先行词，不是定语从句本身。
✓ 正确！"that you recommended" 是定语从句，修饰 the book。
"the book that" 把先行词和关系词混在一起了，不能算一个成分。
es: "I read" es el sujeto y verbo de la oración principal.
"the book" es el antecedente — el sustantivo que la cláusula describe.
✓ ¡Correcto! "that you recommended" es la cláusula relativa que describe "the book."
"the book that" mezcla el antecedente con el inicio de la cláusula — no es una unidad.

q.en:
Which word is the ANTECEDENT (the noun being described)?
The scientist who discovered the vaccine won a prize.
q.zh:
下面哪个词是先行词（被定语从句修饰的名词）？
The scientist who discovered the vaccine won a prize.
q.es:
¿Cuál es el ANTECEDENTE (el sustantivo descrito)?
The scientist who discovered the vaccine won a prize.

t.en: The antecedent is the noun right BEFORE the relative pronoun. Ask: who or what is being described?
t.zh: 先行词就是关系代词前面那个名词。问自己：定语从句在描述谁/什么？
t.es: El antecedente es el sustantivo justo ANTES del pronombre relativo. Pregunta: ¿a quién o qué describe la cláusula?

en: ✓ Correct! "The scientist" is the antecedent — it is the noun that "who discovered the vaccine" describes.
"who discovered" is the beginning of the relative clause, not the noun being described.
"the vaccine" is the object inside the relative clause, not the antecedent.
"won a prize" is the main clause predicate, not the noun being described.
zh: ✓ 正确！"The scientist" 是先行词——定语从句 "who discovered the vaccine" 就是修饰它的。
"who discovered" 是定语从句的开头部分，不是被修饰的名词。
"the vaccine" 是定语从句内部的宾语，不是先行词。
"won a prize" 是主句的谓语，不是被修饰的名词。
es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "The scientist" es el antecedente — la cláusula "who discovered the vaccine" lo describe.
"who discovered" es el inicio de la cláusula relativa, no el sustantivo descrito.
"the vaccine" es el objeto dentro de la cláusula relativa, no el antecedente.
"won a prize" es el predicado principal, no el sustantivo descrito.

q.en:
Identify the relative clause:
The cafe where we met has closed.
q.zh:
找出定语从句：
The cafe where we met has closed.
q.es:
Identifica la cláusula relativa:
The cafe where we met has closed.

t.en: Relative clauses can start with where (for places), when (for times), and why (for reasons).
t.zh: 定语从句也可以用 where（地点）、when（时间）、why（原因）来引导。
t.es: Las cláusulas relativas también pueden usar where (lugar), when (tiempo) y why (razón).

en: "The cafe" is the antecedent — the place being described.
✓ Correct! "where we met" is a relative clause using the relative adverb "where" to describe a place.
"has closed" is the main clause verb, not the relative clause.
"The cafe where" is incomplete — it mixes the antecedent with the start of the clause.
zh: "The cafe" 是先行词，不是定语从句。
✓ 正确！"where we met" 是由关系副词 where 引导的定语从句，修饰地点。
"The cafe where" 不完整——把先行词和关系词混在一起了。
es: "The cafe" es el antecedente — el lugar descrito.
✓ ¡Correcto! "where we met" es una cláusula relativa con el adverbio relativo "where" para describir un lugar.
"has closed" es el verbo principal, no la cláusula relativa.
"The cafe where" está incompleto — mezcla el antecedente con el inicio de la cláusula.

q.en:
Does this sentence contain a relative clause?
She is the person I told you about.
q.zh:
这个句子有定语从句吗？
She is the person I told you about.
q.es:
¿Esta oración contiene una cláusula relativa?
She is the person I told you about.

q.a: Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.

en: No — there is no relative clause.
Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.
Yes — "She is the person" is the relative clause.
No — relative clauses always need who/which/that.
zh: No — there is no relative clause.
Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.
Yes — "She is the person" is the relative clause.
No — relative clauses always need who/which/that.
es: No — there is no relative clause.
Yes — "I told you about" is a relative clause with the pronoun omitted.
Yes — "She is the person" is the relative clause.
No — relative clauses always need who/which/that.

t.en: Object relative pronouns (whom/which/that) can be omitted: "the book (that) I read."
t.zh: 宾语位置的关系代词可以省略，如 "the book (that) I read"。
t.es: Los pronombres relativos objeto pueden omitirse: "the book (that) I read."

en: Look again — there IS a relative clause here, but the pronoun is hidden.
✓ Correct! The full form is "the person (whom) I told you about." English lets you drop the object relative pronoun.
"She is the person" is the main clause, not a relative clause.
English often drops object relative pronouns in speech and informal writing. It's still a relative clause.
zh: 再仔细看——这里确实有定语从句，只是关系代词被省略了。
✓ 正确！完整形式是 "the person (whom) I told you about"。英语中宾语位置的关系代词可以省略。
"She is the person" 是主句，不是定语从句。
英语口语中经常省略宾语位置的关系代词，但这仍然是定语从句。
es: Mira de nuevo — SÍ hay una cláusula relativa, pero el pronombre está oculto.
✓ ¡Correcto! La forma completa es "the person (whom) I told you about." En inglés se puede omitir el pronombre relativo objeto.
"She is the person" es la oración principal, no una cláusula relativa.
En inglés se omiten frecuentemente los pronombres relativos objeto. Sigue siendo una cláusula relativa.

q.en:
Read this sentence:
*The woman she called me is my manager.
What is the problem?
q.zh:
读这个句子：
*The woman she called me is my manager.
问题出在哪？
q.es:
Lee esta oración:
*The woman she called me is my manager.
¿Cuál es el problema?

q.a: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.

en: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.
The sentence needs a comma after "woman."
There is no problem — the sentence is correct.
zh: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.
The sentence needs a comma after "woman."
There is no problem — the sentence is correct.
es: The word "she" is extra — the relative pronoun already represents the woman.
The sentence needs a comma after "woman."
There is no problem — the sentence is correct.

t.en: Chinese speakers often add extra pronouns (resumptive pronouns). In English, the relative pronoun REPLACES the noun — don't add another one.
t.zh: 中文母语者常会多加代词（赘余代词）。英语中关系代词已经代替了那个名词——不要再加一个。
t.es: Los hablantes de chino y español a veces añaden pronombres extra. En inglés, el pronombre relativo REEMPLAZA al sustantivo.

en: ✓ Correct! This is a RESUMPTIVE PRONOUN error. The relative pronoun already stands for "the woman," so "she" is redundant. Fix: "The woman who called me is my manager."
The verb tense is fine. The problem is the extra pronoun.
Adding a comma wouldn't fix the double-subject problem.
The sentence is ungrammatical — a native speaker would notice the repetition immediately.
zh: ✓ 正确！这是"赘余代词"错误。关系代词已经代表了 the woman，再加 she 就重复了。正确：The woman who called me is my manager.
动词时态没问题，问题在于多出来的代词。
加逗号不能解决双重主语的问题。
这个句子不合语法——英语母语者会立刻发现重复了。
es: ✓ ¡Correcto! Es un error de PRONOMBRE RESUNTIVO. El pronombre relativo ya representa a "the woman", así que "she" es redundante. Corrección: "The woman who called me is my manager."
El tiempo verbal está bien. El problema es el pronombre extra.
Añadir una coma no arregla el problema del doble sujeto.
La oración es incorrecta — un hablante nativo notaría la repetición inmediatamente.

s.en: The relative word does the action

d.en: Choose who, which, or that when the relative word is the subject.
d.zh: 当关系词在从句中作主语时，选择 who、which 或 that。
d.es: Elige who, which o that cuando el relativo funciona como sujeto.

q.en:
Choose the correct word:
The woman ___ lives next door is a doctor.
q.zh:
选择正确的词：
The woman ___ lives next door is a doctor.
q.es:
Elige la palabra correcta:
The woman ___ lives next door is a doctor.

t.en: Use WHO for people as subjects. The relative pronoun does double duty: it connects AND acts as the subject.
t.zh: 修饰人且作主语 → 用 who。关系代词身兼两职：连接词 + 主语。
t.es: Usa WHO para personas como sujeto. El pronombre relativo hace doble trabajo: conecta Y actúa como sujeto.

en: ✓ Correct! "who" is used for people when the pronoun is the subject of the relative clause.
"which" is for things, not people. The antecedent "woman" is a person.
"whose" shows possession. We don't need possession here — we need a subject.
"where" is for places. The antecedent is a person, not a location.
zh: ✓ 正确！修饰人且在从句中作主语时用 who。
which 用于修饰物，不能修饰人。
whose 表示所属关系，这里不需要。
where 用于修饰地点，这里修饰的是人。
es: ✓ ¡Correcto! "who" se usa para personas cuando el pronombre es el sujeto de la cláusula relativa.
"which" es para cosas, no para personas.
"whose" indica posesión. Aquí necesitamos un sujeto, no posesión.
"where" es para lugares. El antecedente es una persona.

q.en:
Choose the correct word:
The car ___ is parked outside is mine.
q.zh:
选择正确的词：
The car ___ is parked outside is mine.
q.es:
Elige la palabra correcta:
The car ___ is parked outside is mine.

t.en: For things as subjects, use THAT or WHICH. Both are correct and interchangeable.
t.zh: 修饰物且作主语 → that 或 which 都可以，可以互换。
t.es: Para cosas como sujeto, usa THAT o WHICH. Ambos son correctos e intercambiables.

en: "who" is for people. "Car" is a thing.
"whose" shows possession. We don't need possession — we need a subject pronoun.
✓ Correct! "that" (or "which") is used for things as the subject of the relative clause.
"where" is for places. A car is an object, not a location.
zh: whose 表示所属，这里需要主语关系代词。
✓ 正确！修饰物且在从句中作主语用 that 或 which。
where 修饰地点，car 是物品不是地点。
es: "who" es para personas. "Car" es una cosa.
"whose" indica posesión. Necesitamos un pronombre sujeto.
✓ ¡Correcto! "that" (o "which") se usa para cosas como sujeto de la cláusula relativa.
"where" es para lugares. Un coche es un objeto, no un lugar.

q.en:
Find the mistake:
*The book which is on the table belong to me.
q.zh:
找出错误：
*The book which is on the table belong to me.
q.es:
Encuentra el error:
*The book which is on the table belong to me.

q.a: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).

en: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).
"on the table" should be "at the table."
zh: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).
"on the table" should be "at the table."
es: "belong" should be "belongs" — the verb must agree with "book" (singular).
"on the table" should be "at the table."

t.en: The main verb must agree with the ANTECEDENT, not with the relative clause. "The book...belongs" (singular).
t.zh: 主句动词要和先行词保持一致，不是和定语从句一致。The book...belongs（单数）。
t.es: El verbo principal concuerda con el ANTECEDENTE: "The book...belongs" (singular).

en: "which" is correct here — "book" is a thing. The error is elsewhere.
✓ Correct! The main verb must agree with the antecedent "book" (singular), so it should be "belongs."
"on the table" is correct — the book is resting on the surface.
The sentence has a subject-verb agreement error: "book" is singular, "belong" is plural.
zh: which 修饰 book 是正确的，错误不在这里。
✓ 正确！主句动词必须与先行词 book（单数）一致，所以应该是 belongs。
on the table 是正确的。
句子有主谓一致错误：book 是单数，belong 是复数形式。
es: "which" es correcto aquí — "book" es una cosa. El error está en otra parte.
✓ ¡Correcto! El verbo principal debe concordar con "book" (singular), debe ser "belongs."
Hay un error de concordancia: "book" es singular, "belong" es plural.

q.en:
Choose the best option:
People ___ exercise regularly live longer.
q.zh:
选择最佳选项：
People ___ exercise regularly live longer.
q.es:
Elige la mejor opción:
People ___ exercise regularly live longer.

t.en: People = who (subject). Things = which/that. This is the most basic relative pronoun rule.
t.zh: 人 → who（主语），物 → which/that。这是最基本的关系代词规则。
t.es: Personas = who (sujeto). Cosas = which/that. Es la regla más básica de pronombres relativos.

en: "which" is for things. "People" are human beings.
✓ Correct! "who" is used when the antecedent is a person and the pronoun is the subject.
"whose" would need a noun after it (e.g., "whose habits"). It shows possession.
"whom" is for objects. Here "people" is the subject of "exercise."
zh: ✓ 正确！先行词是人且在从句中作主语时用 who。
whose 后面需要名词（如 whose habits），表示所属关系。
whom 作宾语，这里 people 是 exercise 的主语。
es: "which" es para cosas. "People" son seres humanos.
✓ ¡Correcto! "who" se usa cuando el antecedente es una persona y el pronombre es el sujeto.
"whose" necesitaría un sustantivo después. Indica posesión.
"whom" es para objetos. Aquí "people" es el sujeto de "exercise."

s.en: The relative word receives the action

d.en: Recognize object relatives and when the relative pronoun may be omitted.
d.zh: 识别宾语关系从句，并判断何时可以省略关系代词。
d.es: Reconoce relativas de objeto y cuándo puede omitirse el pronombre.

q.en: The file ___ you sent is open.
q.zh: The file ___ you sent is open.
q.es: The file ___ you sent is open.

t.en: Object relatives: that/which. Or omit: The file you sent...
t.zh: 宾语关系代词可省略

en: Correct! that for things as object
zh: 正确！that 用于物作宾语

q.en: The movie ___ we watched was boring.
q.zh: The movie ___ we watched was boring.
q.es: The movie ___ we watched was boring.

t.en: Object relatives: that/which for things. Can be omitted: The movie we watched...
t.zh: 宾语关系代词：that/which。可省略。

en: Correct! that/which for things as object
zh: 正确！that/which 用于物作宾语

q.en: The client ___ we met is from Tokyo.
q.zh: The client ___ we met is from Tokyo.
q.es: The client ___ we met is from Tokyo.

t.en: People as objects: whom/that. Or omit the pronoun.
t.zh: 人作宾语可省略关系代词

q.en: The advice ___ she gave me was helpful.
q.zh: The advice ___ she gave me was helpful.
q.es: The advice ___ she gave me was helpful.

t.en: advice is the object of gave. that/which or omit.
t.zh: advice 是 gave 的宾语。用 that/which 或省略。
t.es: advice es objeto. that/which o omitir.

en: Correct! that/which — advice is a thing, object of gave
zh: 正确！advice 是 gave 的宾语
es: Correcto! advice es objeto de gave

q.en: The people ___ I invited are coming.
q.zh: The people ___ I invited are coming.
q.es: The people ___ I invited are coming.

t.en: People as objects -> whom/that. Or omit: The people I invited...
t.zh: 人作宾语 -> whom/that。或省略。

q.en:
Can we drop the pronoun?
The cake ___ I baked was delicious.
q.zh:
可以省略关系代词吗？
The cake ___ I baked was delicious.
q.es:
Se puede omitir?
The cake ___ I baked was delicious.

q.a: Yes — object pronouns can be omitted

en: Yes — object pronouns can be omitted
zh: Yes — object pronouns can be omitted
es: Yes — object pronouns can be omitted

t.en: Object pronouns optional. The cake I baked = The cake that I baked.
t.zh: 宾语关系代词可选。省略后更自然。

en: Object pronouns CAN be dropped in English
Correct! The cake I baked = perfectly fine
Dropping is MORE common in speech
Both that and which can be dropped
zh: 英语中可以省略宾语关系代词
正确！The cake I baked = 完全正确
that 和 which 都可以省略
es: Correcto! The cake I baked = correcto
Omitir es MAS comun al hablar

s.es: Lee la estructura de la cláusula

d.en: Distinguish subject relatives from object relatives by checking for an existing subject.
d.zh: 通过判断从句中是否已有主语，区分主语关系从句和宾语关系从句。
d.es: Distingue relativas de sujeto y objeto comprobando si ya existe un sujeto.

q.en: who called me — subject or object?
q.zh: who called me — 主语还是宾语？
q.es: who called me — sujeto u objeto?

t.en: No other subject -> relative pronoun IS the subject.
t.zh: 没有其他主语->关系代词就是主语
t.es: Sin otro sujeto -> el relativo ES el sujeto.

zh: 正确！who 是发出动作者

q.en: The man ___ she married is a chef. Subject or object?
q.zh: The man ___ she married is a chef. 主语还是宾语？
q.es: The man ___ she married... sujeto u objeto?

t.en: she is subject -> relative pronoun must be object.
t.zh: she 是主语 -> 关系代词必为宾语。
t.es: she es sujeto -> relativo es objeto.

en: If subject, no other subject in clause
zh: 如果是主语，从句中没有其他主语
正确！she=主语，关系代词=宾语
es: Si sujeto, no hay otro sujeto

q.en: who I called — subject or object?
q.zh: who I called — 主语还是宾语？
q.es: who I called — sujeto u objeto?

t.en: I is subject -> relative pronoun must be object.
t.zh: I 是主语->关系代词必为宾语
t.es: I es sujeto -> el relativo es objeto.

zh: 正确！who 接受 call 这个动作

q.en: Fix: The man who he called me is my neighbor.
q.zh: 改正：The man who he called me... 去掉哪个词？
q.es: Corrige: The man who he called me...

t.en: who represents the antecedent. No need for he/she/it.
t.zh: who 已经代表先行词。不需要 he/she/it。
t.es: who representa el antecedente. No necesita he/she/it.

en: Correct! he is extra — who already represents the man
zh: 正确！he 多余 — who 已经代表那个人
es: Correcto! he sobra — who ya representa al hombre

q.en: Fix: The report that Anna wrote it is ready.
q.zh: 改正：The report that Anna wrote it... 去掉哪个词？
q.es: Corrige: The report that Anna wrote it...

t.en: Resumptive pronoun: common Chinese-L1 error. that already = the report.
t.zh: 赘余代词：中文母语者常见错误。that 已经 = the report。
t.es: Pronombre resuntivo: error comun. that ya = the report.

en: Correct! it is redundant — that already = the report
zh: 正确！it 多余 — that 已经 = the report
es: Correcto! it sobra — that ya = the report

q.en: The student ___ answered correctly won. Subject or object?
q.zh: The student ___ answered correctly... 主语还是宾语？
q.es: The student ___ answered... sujeto u objeto?

t.en: No noun after blank -> relative pronoun is the subject.
t.zh: 空格后没有名词 -> 关系代词是主语。
t.es: Sin sustantivo despues -> relativo es sujeto.

en: Correct! no other subject -> relative pronoun IS subject
If object, another noun/pronoun would do the answering
zh: 正确！没有其他主语 -> 关系代词就是主语
如果是宾语，应该有另一个名词/代词发出动作
es: Correcto! sin otro sujeto -> relativo ES sujeto
Si objeto, otro sustantivo haria la accion

s.en: where, when, why and preposition forms
s.es: where, when, why y formas con preposición

d.en: Use where, when, why, and equivalent preposition-based relative forms.
d.zh: 使用 where、when、why 以及对应的介词关系结构。
d.es: Usa where, when, why y sus equivalentes con preposición.

q.en: This is the cafe ___ we met.
q.zh: This is the cafe ___ we met.
q.es: This is the cafe ___ we met.

t.en: where = in which. For places.
t.zh: where = 地点

q.en: The office ___ I work is downtown.
q.zh: The office ___ I work is downtown.
q.es: The office ___ I work is downtown.

t.en: where = in/at which. The office in which I work.
t.zh: where = 在其中。工作的地方。

q.en: Friday is the day ___ we present.
q.zh: Friday is the day ___ we present.
q.es: Friday is the day ___ we present.

t.en: when = on which. For times.
t.zh: when = 时间

q.en: That is the reason ___ I left early.
q.zh: That is the reason ___ I left early.
q.es: That is the reason ___ I left early.

t.en: why = for which reason. The reason for which I left.
t.zh: why = 因为那个原因。

q.en: The year ___ I graduated was 2020.
q.zh: The year ___ I graduated was 2020.
q.es: The year ___ I graduated was 2020.

t.en: when = in which. The year in which I graduated.
t.zh: when = 在那时。毕业的那一年。

q.en: This is the room ___ the meeting will happen.
q.zh: This is the room ___ the meeting will happen.
q.es: This is the room ___ the meeting will happen.

t.en: where = in which. Room = place -> where.
t.zh: where = 地点。房间 -> where。

s.es: Expresa posesión dentro de la cláusula

d.en: Use whose naturally for people, organizations, animals, and things.
d.zh: 自然地用 whose 表达人、组织、动物和事物的所属关系。
d.es: Usa whose de forma natural con personas, organizaciones, animales y cosas.

q.en: The man ___ car was stolen reported it.
q.zh: The man ___ car was stolen reported it.
q.es: The man ___ car was stolen reported it.

t.en: whose = possession. His car = whose car.
t.zh: whose = 所属。他的车 = whose car。
t.es: whose = posesión. Su coche = whose car.

en: who is for subjects, not possession

q.en: The company ___ CEO resigned is in trouble.
q.zh: The company ___ CEO resigned is in trouble.
q.es: La empresa ___ CEO renuncio tiene problemas.

t.en: whose works for things too! The company whose CEO...
t.zh: whose 也可以用于物！The company whose CEO...

en: that works but less precise for possession

q.en: I know a woman ___ son is a pilot.
q.zh: I know a woman ___ son is a pilot.
q.es: Conozco a una mujer ___ hijo es piloto.

t.en: whose works for people AND things. The woman whose son...
t.zh: whose 可用于人和物。The woman whose son...
t.es: whose funciona para personas Y cosas.

en: that works but whose is more precise
zh: that 也可以但 whose 更准确
es: that funciona pero whose es más preciso

q.en: The tree ___ branches fell was very old.
q.zh: The tree ___ branches fell was very old.
q.es: El arbol ___ ramas cayeron era muy viejo.

t.en: whose = of which. The tree, the branches of which...
t.zh: whose = 它的。树的树枝...
t.es: whose = cuyas. Las ramas del arbol...

en: that works but whose is better
zh: that 可以但 whose 更好

q.en: That is the writer ___ books I love.
q.zh: That is the writer ___ books I love.
q.es: Ese es el escritor ___ libros adoro.

t.en: whose = possessive. His books, her son, its branches.
t.zh: whose = 所属关系。他的书，她的儿子，它的树枝。
t.es: whose = posesivo. Sus libros, su hijo, sus ramas.

en: which is for things, not possession
zh: which 用于物，不表示所属
es: which es para cosas, no posesión

q.en: She adopted a dog ___ owner had passed away.
q.zh: She adopted a dog ___ owner had passed away.
q.es: Adopto un perro ___ dueno habia fallecido.

t.en: whose for people AND animals AND things. The dog whose owner...
t.zh: whose 用于人、动物和物。
t.es: whose para personas, animales y cosas.

en: that works for animals but whose is more specific
zh: that 可以但 whose 更精确
es: that funciona pero whose mas especifico

d.en: Choose punctuation and relative words according to essential or extra information.
d.zh: 根据信息是必要还是补充，选择标点和关系词。
d.es: Elige puntuación y relativos según la información sea esencial o adicional.

q.en: The students who passed celebrated. (only some passed) Comma?
q.zh: The students who passed celebrated.（只有部分通过）加逗号吗？
q.es: The students who passed celebrated. (solo algunos) ¿Coma?

t.en: No comma = restrictive (essential info). Comma = non-restrictive (extra info).
t.zh: 不加逗号 = 限制性（必要信息）。加逗号 = 非限制性（补充信息）。
t.es: Sin coma = restrictiva. Con coma = no restrictiva.

en: Correct! Restrictive = no comma. We need to identify WHICH students.
Comma would mean ALL students passed.
zh: 正确！限制性 = 不加逗号。需要区分哪些学生。
加逗号意味着所有学生都通过了。
es: Correcto! Restrictiva = sin coma. Necesitamos identificar QUÉ estudiantes.
La coma significaría que TODOS pasaron.

q.en: The car which is parked outside is mine. (several cars) comma?
q.zh: The car which is parked outside...（外面好几辆）逗号？
q.es: The car which is parked... (varios) coma?

t.en: Several cars outside -> need to identify WHICH one -> no comma.
t.zh: 外面好几辆 -> 需要指定是哪辆 -> 不加逗号。

en: Correct! need to specify which car -> restrictive -> no comma
zh: 正确！需要指定哪辆 -> 限制性 -> 不加逗号
逗号 = 所有车都在外面
es: Correcto! hay que especificar -> restrictiva -> sin coma

q.en: My brother who lives in Tokyo is visiting. (I have one brother) Comma?
q.zh: My brother who lives in Tokyo is visiting.（只有一个哥哥）加逗号吗？
q.es: My brother who lives in Tokyo... (un hermano) ¿Coma?

t.en: One brother = comma (extra info). Multiple brothers = no comma (identifying which one).
t.zh: 一个 = 加逗号。多个 = 不加逗号。

en: Only ONE brother — no need to identify which one.
Correct! Non-restrictive = comma. The info is extra.
zh: 只有一个哥哥——不需要区分是哪一个。
正确！非限制性 = 加逗号。这是补充信息。
es: Solo UN hermano — no hay que identificar cuál.
Correcto! No restrictiva = coma. Es información extra.

q.en: Dr. Chen, who has 20 years exp., will lead. comma?
q.zh: Dr. Chen... 加逗号吗？
q.es: Dr. Chen... coma?

t.en: Known person = non-restrictive = comma. Extra, not essential.
t.zh: 已知人物 = 非限制性 = 加逗号。补充信息。

en: No comma = need to identify which Dr. Chen
Correct! name is known -> extra info -> comma
zh: 不加逗号 = 需要区分哪个陈医生
正确！名字已知 -> 补充信息 -> 加逗号
es: Sin coma = hay que identificar cual
Correcto! nombre conocido -> info extra -> coma

q.en: A doctor who has 20 years exp. will lead. comma?
q.zh: A doctor... 加逗号吗？
q.es: A doctor... coma?

t.en: A doctor (unknown which) = restrictive = no comma.
t.zh: 一个医生（未指定）= 限制性 = 不加逗号。

en: Correct! need to specify which doctor -> restrictive -> no comma
Comma = we already know which doctor
zh: 正确！需要指定哪个医生 -> 限制性 -> 不加逗号
逗号 = 已经知道是哪个医生
es: Correcto! hay que especificar -> restrictiva -> sin coma

q.en: The managers who attended agreed. vs The managers, who attended, agreed.
q.zh: 对比两句的意思：哪个表示"所有经理都参加了"？
q.es: Cual significa todos asistieron?

t.en: Comma changes meaning: who attended (some) vs ,who attended, (all).
t.zh: 逗号改变意思：who attended（部分）vs ,who attended,（全部）。

en: No comma = only those who attended (restrictive)
zh: 不加逗号 = 只有参会的经理
正确！加逗号 = 所有经理都参会了
es: Sin coma = solo los que asistieron

d.en: Apply all relative-clause patterns in mixed editing and sentence-building tasks.
d.zh: 在混合改错和造句任务中综合运用所有定语从句结构。
d.es: Aplica todos los patrones en tareas mixtas de corrección y producción.

q.en: Combine: A customer called. She ordered yesterday.
q.zh: 合并：A customer called. She ordered yesterday.
q.es: Combina: A customer called. She ordered yesterday.

q.a: The customer who ordered yesterday called.

en: The customer who ordered yesterday called.
The customer called who ordered yesterday.
A customer called she ordered yesterday.
zh: The customer who ordered yesterday called.
The customer called who ordered yesterday.
A customer called she ordered yesterday.
es: The customer who ordered yesterday called.
The customer called who ordered yesterday.
A customer called she ordered yesterday.

t.en: Merge by replacing the repeated noun with a relative pronoun.
t.zh: 用关系代词替换重复的名词来合并。
t.es: Reemplaza el sustantivo repetido con un pronombre relativo.

en: Correct! The relative clause goes right after the noun it describes.
Wrong word order — the clause must follow the antecedent.
zh: 正确！定语从句紧跟被修饰的名词。
语序错误——从句必须跟在先行词后。
es: Correcto! La cláusula va justo después del sustantivo.

q.en: Combine: We visited a museum. It has ancient art.
q.zh: 合并：We visited a museum. It has ancient art.
q.es: Combina: We visited a museum. It has ancient art.

q.a: We visited a museum that has ancient art.

en: We visited a museum that has ancient art.
We visited a museum it has ancient art.
We visited that has ancient art a museum.
zh: We visited a museum that has ancient art.
We visited a museum it has ancient art.
We visited that has ancient art a museum.
es: We visited a museum that has ancient art.
We visited a museum it has ancient art.
We visited that has ancient art a museum.

t.en: Replace the repeated word with a relative pronoun.
t.zh: 用关系代词替换重复的词。

q.en: Combine: I found a book. It explains grammar.
q.zh: 合并：I found a book. It explains grammar.
q.es: Combina: I found a book. It explains grammar.

q.a: I found a book which explains grammar.

en: I found a book which explains grammar.
I found a book it explains grammar.
I found which explains grammar a book.
zh: I found a book which explains grammar.
I found a book it explains grammar.
I found which explains grammar a book.
es: I found a book which explains grammar.
I found a book it explains grammar.
I found which explains grammar a book.

t.en: Replace the repeated noun/pronoun with a relative pronoun.
t.zh: 用关系代词替换重复的名词/代词。

en: Correct! which/that replaces it and connects the sentences.
Extra it — the relative pronoun already replaces it.
Wrong position — the clause must follow the antecedent.
zh: 正确！which/that 代替 it 并连接两个句子。
多余的 it——关系代词已经代替了它。
位置错误——从句必须跟在先行词后。
es: Correcto! which/that reemplaza it y conecta las oraciones.

q.en: Reorder: The package arrived today that I ordered.
q.zh: 调整语序：The package arrived today that I ordered.
q.es: Reordena: The package arrived today that I ordered.

q.a: The package that I ordered arrived today.

en: The package that I ordered arrived today.
The package arrived today that I ordered.
The package that arrived today I ordered.
zh: The package that I ordered arrived today.
The package arrived today that I ordered.
The package that arrived today I ordered.
es: The package that I ordered arrived today.
The package arrived today that I ordered.
The package that arrived today I ordered.

t.en: The relative clause should be NEXT to the noun it describes.
t.zh: 定语从句应紧挨被修饰的名词。
t.es: La clausula debe estar JUNTO al sustantivo.

en: Correct! the clause should be right after the antecedent
Clause too far from antecedent — awkward
zh: 正确！从句应紧跟先行词
es: Correcto! clausula justo despues del antecedente

q.en: Fix: This is the hotel where I recommended it.
q.zh: 改正：This is the hotel where I recommended it.
q.es: Corrige: This is the hotel where I recommended it.

q.a: This is the hotel that I recommended.

en: This is the hotel that I recommended.
This is the hotel where I recommended.
This is the hotel where I recommended it.
zh: This is the hotel that I recommended.
This is the hotel where I recommended.
This is the hotel where I recommended it.
es: This is the hotel that I recommended.
This is the hotel where I recommended.
This is the hotel where I recommended it.

t.en: where = place you do something AT. Recommend takes a direct object -> that/which.
t.zh: where = 做某事的地点。Recommend 接直接宾语 -> that/which。
t.es: where = lugar DONDE. Recommend lleva objeto directo -> that/which.

en: Correct! recommend + hotel -> that/which, not where
where is wrong — hotel is object of recommend, not a place where you recommend
Original with extra it — wrong on two counts
zh: where 错误 — hotel 是 recommend 的宾语

q.en: Combine using a relative clause: I met a designer. She created our website.
q.zh: 用定语从句合并：I met a designer. She created our website.
q.es: Combina: I met a designer. She created our website.

q.a: I met a designer who created our website.

en: I met a designer who created our website.
I met who created our website a designer.
I met a designer she created our website.
zh: I met a designer who created our website.
I met who created our website a designer.
I met a designer she created our website.
es: I met a designer who created our website.
I met who created our website a designer.
I met a designer she created our website.

t.en: who + verb (created). Relative pronoun IS the subject.
t.zh: who + 动词。关系代词 = 主语。

en: Correct! who replaces she and joins the sentences
Extra she — already replaced by who
zh: 正确！who 代替 she 并连接句子

d.en: Distinguish in, on, and at for physical and conceptual locations.
d.zh: 区分 in、on、at 在具体和抽象地点中的用法。
d.es: Distingue in, on y at para lugares físicos y conceptuales.

q.en: The cat is sleeping ___ the box.
q.zh: The cat is sleeping ___ the box.
q.es: El gato duerme ___ la caja.

t.en: in = inside a 3D space.
t.zh: in = 三维空间内

q.en: There is a spider on the ceiling.
q.zh: There is a spider on the ceiling.
q.es: Hay una arana en el techo.

t.en: on = touching the surface.
t.zh: on = 接触表面。

q.en: The book is ___ the table.
q.zh: The book is ___ the table.
q.es: El libro esta ___ la mesa.

t.en: on = contact with surface.
t.zh: on = 接触表面

q.en: She is waiting at the door.
q.zh: She is waiting at the door.
q.es: Ella espera en la puerta.

t.en: at = specific point. At the door, at the station.
t.zh: at = 具体位置。

q.en: The kids are playing in the garden.
q.zh: The kids are playing in the garden.
q.es: Los ninos juegan en el jardin.

t.en: in = inside an area. Garden, park, city.
t.zh: in = 区域内。

q.en: I met her at the bus stop.
q.zh: I met her at the bus stop.
q.es: La conoci en la parada.

t.en: at = meeting points. At the stop, at the corner.
t.zh: at = 会面地点。

d.en: Choose in, on, or at according to the size and type of time expression.
d.zh: 根据时间表达的范围和类型选择 in、on 或 at。
d.es: Elige in, on o at según el tamaño y tipo de expresión temporal.

q.en: The meeting starts ___ 3 PM.
q.zh: The meeting starts ___ 3 PM.
q.es: La reunion empieza ___ las 3 PM.

t.en: at = precise time.
t.zh: at = 精确时刻

q.en: She arrived on Monday morning.
q.zh: She arrived on Monday morning.
q.es: Llego el lunes por la manana.

t.en: on = days. Monday, my birthday.
t.zh: on = 星期/日期。

q.en: I was born ___ July.
q.zh: I was born ___ July.
q.es: Naci ___ julio.

t.en: in = months, years, seasons.
t.zh: in = 月份、年份、季节

q.en: We go skiing in winter.
q.zh: We go skiing in winter.
q.es: Esquiamos en invierno.

t.en: in = seasons and long periods.
t.zh: in = 季节。

q.en: The train leaves at noon.
q.zh: The train leaves at noon.
q.es: El tren sale al mediodia.

t.en: at = noon, midnight, dawn. Clock points.
t.zh: at = noon/midnight。精确时间点。

q.en: Her birthday is on May 15th.
q.zh: Her birthday is on May 15th.
q.es: Su cumpleanos es el 15 de mayo.

t.en: on = specific dates. July 4th, May 15th.
t.zh: on = 具体日期。

d.en: Switch between place and time meanings without relying on lesson labels.
d.zh: 在没有课程标签提示的情况下切换地点和时间用法。
d.es: Alterna entre lugar y tiempo sin depender de la etiqueta de la lección.

q.en: She lives ___ New York.
q.zh: She lives ___ New York.
q.es: Ella vive ___ Nueva York.

t.en: First: place or time? Then: apply rule.
t.zh: 先判断地点还是时间

q.en: He is standing at the corner.
q.zh: He is standing at the corner.
q.es: El esta en la esquina.

t.en: at the corner = intersection point.
t.zh: at the corner = 街角。

q.en: See you ___ Friday.
q.zh: See you ___ Friday.
q.es: Nos vemos ___ el viernes.

t.en: on = days and dates.
t.zh: on = 星期和日期

q.en: Put the flowers in the vase.
q.zh: Put the flowers in the vase.
q.es: Pon las flores en el florero.

t.en: in = containment. Inside a container.
t.zh: in = 容器内。

d.en: Correct and produce sentences using in, on, and at in realistic contexts.
d.zh: 在真实语境中改正并产出使用 in、on、at 的句子。
d.es: Corrige y produce oraciones con in, on y at en contextos reales.

q.en: Fix: I was born in July 15th.
q.zh: 改正：I was born in July 15th.
q.es: Corrige: I was born in July 15th.

t.en: Specific date -> on. Month alone -> in.
t.zh: 具体日期用 on

q.en: Fix: She arrived at Monday.
q.zh: 改正：She arrived at Monday.
q.es: Corrige: She arrived at Monday.

t.en: Days -> on. Monday = on.
t.zh: 星期 -> on。

q.en: Fill: Hang the painting ___ the wall.
q.zh: 填空：Hang the painting ___ the wall.
q.es: Completa: Hang the painting ___ the wall.

t.en: on = surface contact.
t.zh: on = 表面接触

q.en: We stayed at a hotel.
q.zh: We stayed at a hotel.
q.es: Nos quedamos en un hotel.

t.en: at = hotel, restaurant, airport, school.
t.zh: at = 场所。

d.en: Choose prepositions that show destination, entry, surface movement, passage, and crossing.
d.zh: 选择表示目的地、进入、到达表面、穿过和横跨的介词。
d.es: Elige preposiciones de destino, entrada, superficie, paso y cruce.

s.es: El significado está en la combinación

d.en: Use common verb-preposition combinations and distinguish near alternatives.
d.zh: 使用常见动词介词搭配，并区分相近表达。
d.es: Usa combinaciones frecuentes y distingue alternativas cercanas.

d.en: Use adjective-preposition patterns for feelings, opinions, and relationships.
d.zh: 使用形容词介词搭配表达感受、观点和关系。
d.es: Usa patrones de adjetivo y preposición para sentimientos, opiniones y relaciones.

s.zh: 无主题提示的混合语境

d.en: Apply place, time, movement, and fixed patterns in mixed real-world tasks.
d.zh: 在真实混合任务中运用地点、时间、移动和固定搭配。
d.es: Aplica lugar, tiempo, movimiento y patrones fijos en tareas reales.

d.en: Use the present simple for routines, facts, schedules, and stable states.
d.zh: 用一般现在时表达日常习惯、事实、日程和稳定状态。
d.es: Usa el presente simple para rutinas, hechos, horarios y estados estables.

d.en: Use the present continuous for current, temporary, arranged, and changing situations.
d.zh: 用现在进行时表达当前、暂时、已安排和正在变化的情况。
d.es: Usa el presente continuo para situaciones actuales, temporales, concertadas y cambiantes.

d.en: Use the past simple for completed events in a finished past time.
d.zh: 用一般过去时表达发生在已结束时间中的完成事件。
d.es: Usa el pasado simple para eventos completos en un tiempo pasado terminado.

d.en: Use the past continuous for background actions, overlap, and interruption.
d.zh: 用过去进行时表达背景动作、同时进行和被打断的动作。
d.es: Usa el pasado continuo para acciones de fondo, simultáneas e interrumpidas.

d.en: Choose will, be going to, or present continuous by evidence and intention.
d.zh: 根据证据和意图选择 will、be going to 或现在进行时。
d.es: Elige will, be going to o presente continuo según evidencia e intención.

d.en: Use the present perfect for experience, unfinished time, result, and duration.
d.zh: 用现在完成时表达经历、未结束时间、结果和持续。
d.es: Usa el presente perfecto para experiencia, tiempo no terminado, resultado y duración.

d.en: Use the past perfect to make the earlier past event clear.
d.zh: 用过去完成时明确表示较早发生的过去事件。
d.es: Usa el pluscuamperfecto para aclarar cuál evento pasado ocurrió antes.

s.es: Lee el significado antes de la forma

d.en: Select and edit tense choices across mixed narratives and conversations.
d.zh: 在混合叙事和对话中选择并修改时态。
d.es: Selecciona y corrige tiempos en narraciones y conversaciones mixtas.

d.en: Use zero conditionals for general truths, routines, and automatic results.
d.zh: 用零条件句表达普遍事实、习惯和必然结果。
d.es: Usa el condicional cero para verdades generales, rutinas y resultados automáticos.

d.en: Use first conditionals for realistic future possibilities and consequences.
d.zh: 用第一条件句表达真实可行的未来可能和结果。
d.es: Usa el primer condicional para posibilidades y consecuencias futuras realistas.

d.en: Use second conditionals for imagined, unlikely, or advisory situations.
d.zh: 用第二条件句表达假设、不太可能或建议性的情境。
d.es: Usa el segundo condicional para situaciones imaginadas, improbables o de consejo.

d.en: Use third conditionals for past situations that did not happen.
d.zh: 用第三条件句表达过去未发生的情况及其假想结果。
d.es: Usa el tercer condicional para situaciones pasadas que no ocurrieron.

d.en: Connect unreal past causes with present results and present states with past results.
d.zh: 连接虚拟过去原因与现在结果，或现在状态与过去结果。
d.es: Conecta causas irreales pasadas con resultados presentes y viceversa.

s.es: Expresa la condición de otra forma

d.en: Use unless, provided, as long as, even if, and only if accurately.
d.zh: 准确使用 unless、provided、as long as、even if 和 only if。
d.es: Usa con precisión unless, provided, as long as, even if y only if.

s.zh: 不用 if 的条件句

d.en: Recognize and use had, were, and should inversion in formal conditions.
d.zh: 识别并使用 had、were、should 倒装构成的正式条件句。
d.es: Reconoce y usa inversión con had, were y should en condiciones formales.

s.zh: 判断真实性、时间与结果

d.en: Apply conditional forms in advice, negotiation, explanation, and reflection.
d.zh: 在建议、谈判、解释和反思中综合运用条件句。
d.es: Aplica condicionales en consejos, negociación, explicación y reflexión.

s.en: Choose what the sentence is about
s.es: Elige de qué trata la oración

d.en: Decide whether the agent, action, or result should be the grammatical focus.
d.zh: 判断施事者、动作或结果哪个应成为句子焦点。
d.es: Decide si el agente, la acción o el resultado debe ser el foco gramatical.

d.en: Form and use present passive structures in descriptions and processes.
d.zh: 在描述和流程中构成并使用一般现在时被动。
d.es: Forma y usa la pasiva presente en descripciones y procesos.

s.zh: 关注结果的已完成事件
s.es: Eventos terminados con foco en el resultado

d.en: Use past passive forms for completed events when the agent is secondary or unknown.
d.zh: 当施事者次要或未知时，用一般过去时被动表达完成事件。
d.es: Usa la pasiva pasada para eventos completos cuando el agente es secundario o desconocido.

s.en: What will or must be done
s.es: Lo que será o deberá hacerse

d.en: Build passive forms with will and modal verbs.
d.zh: 构成含 will 和情态动词的被动结构。
d.es: Construye pasivas con will y verbos modales.

s.en: Completed action with present or past relevance
s.zh: 与现在或过去相关的完成动作
s.es: Acción completada con relevancia presente o pasada

d.en: Use present-perfect and past-perfect passive forms accurately.
d.zh: 准确使用现在完成时和过去完成时被动。
d.es: Usa correctamente la pasiva en presente perfecto y pluscuamperfecto.

name.zh: 施事者与 by 短语

s.en: Include the doer only when useful
s.zh: 仅在有用时说明施事者
s.es: Incluye al agente solo cuando aporta

d.en: Decide when to include, omit, or foreground the agent.
d.zh: 判断何时保留、省略或突出施事者。
d.es: Decide cuándo incluir, omitir o destacar al agente.

s.en: It is said / He is believed

d.en: Use impersonal and personal reporting passive patterns.
d.zh: 使用无人称和人称报告型被动结构。
d.es: Usa patrones pasivos de reporte impersonales y personales.

d.en: Choose and produce active or passive forms across news, science, and everyday contexts.
d.zh: 在新闻、科学和日常语境中选择并产出主动或被动结构。
d.es: Elige y produce activa o pasiva en noticias, ciencia y contextos cotidianos.

d.en: Report statements with appropriate tense, pronoun, and reference changes.
d.zh: 使用恰当的时态、代词和指代变化转述陈述。
d.es: Informa declaraciones con cambios adecuados de tiempo, pronombre y referencia.

s.es: Las preguntas pasan a orden afirmativo

d.en: Report yes/no and wh-questions using statement word order.
d.zh: 用陈述语序转述一般疑问句和特殊疑问句。
d.es: Informa preguntas de sí/no y de wh con orden afirmativo.

d.en: Report commands, requests, warnings, and advice with infinitive patterns.
d.zh: 用不定式结构转述命令、请求、警告和建议。
d.es: Informa órdenes, peticiones, advertencias y consejos con infinitivos.

s.en: Move the viewpoint into the past
s.es: Mueve el punto de vista al pasado

d.en: Choose when backshift is required, optional, or inappropriate.
d.zh: 判断何时需要、可以或不应进行时态后移。
d.es: Decide cuándo el cambio de tiempo es obligatorio, opcional o inadecuado.

d.en: Adjust pronouns and expressions such as now, today, here, and this.
d.zh: 调整代词以及 now、today、here、this 等表达。
d.es: Ajusta pronombres y expresiones como now, today, here y this.

d.en: Select reporting verbs and their required complement patterns.
d.zh: 选择转述动词及其所要求的补语结构。
d.es: Elige verbos de reporte y los complementos que exigen.

d.en: Preserve certainty, attitude, and responsibility when reporting speech.
d.zh: 转述时保留确定程度、态度和责任归属。
d.es: Conserva certeza, actitud y responsabilidad al informar el discurso.

d.en: Transform dialogues, messages, interviews, and reports into natural reported speech.
d.zh: 把对话、消息、采访和报道自然地转化为间接引语。
d.es: Transforma diálogos, mensajes, entrevistas e informes en estilo indirecto natural.

s.en: Can, could, and be able to
s.es: Can, could y be able to

d.en: Express present, past, general, and achieved ability.
d.zh: 表达现在、过去、一般能力和成功完成某事的能力。
d.es: Expresa capacidad presente, pasada, general y lograda.

s.zh: 礼貌地请求、允许和拒绝
s.es: Pide, permite y rechaza con cortesía

d.en: Choose modal forms by formality, authority, and politeness.
d.zh: 根据正式程度、权力关系和礼貌程度选择情态形式。
d.es: Elige modales según formalidad, autoridad y cortesía.

s.es: Should, ought to y had better

d.en: Distinguish gentle advice, expectation, and strong warning.
d.zh: 区分温和建议、预期和强烈警告。
d.es: Distingue consejo suave, expectativa y advertencia fuerte.

s.es: Must, have to y need to

d.en: Express internal, external, present, and past necessity.
d.zh: 表达内在、外在、现在和过去的必要性。
d.es: Expresa necesidad interna, externa, presente y pasada.

d.en: Rank possible explanations from uncertain to highly probable.
d.zh: 按不确定到高度可能排列和使用情态表达。
d.es: Ordena explicaciones desde inciertas hasta muy probables.

d.en: Use modal deduction about present situations and completed past events.
d.zh: 使用情态动词推断现在情况和已完成的过去事件。
d.es: Usa modales para deducir situaciones presentes y hechos pasados.

s.en: Could have, should have, must have
s.es: Could have, should have, must have

d.en: Express missed possibilities, criticism, regret, and past certainty.
d.zh: 表达错失的可能、批评、遗憾和对过去的确定推断。
d.es: Expresa posibilidades perdidas, crítica, arrepentimiento y certeza pasada.

d.en: Choose modals in realistic workplace, social, and problem-solving contexts.
d.zh: 在工作、社交和解决问题的真实语境中选择情态动词。
d.es: Elige modales en contextos laborales, sociales y de resolución de problemas.

d.en: Use a or an according to sound, countability, and first mention.
d.zh: 根据发音、可数性和首次提及选择 a 或 an。
d.es: Usa a o an según sonido, contabilidad y primera mención.

d.en: Use the when speaker and listener can identify the same referent.
d.zh: 当说话者和听者能识别同一对象时使用 the。
d.es: Usa the cuando hablante y oyente pueden identificar el mismo referente.

s.en: Talk generally or use fixed patterns
s.es: Habla en general o usa patrones fijos

d.en: Omit articles with general plurals, mass nouns, institutions, and fixed expressions.
d.zh: 在泛指复数、物质名词、机构和固定表达中省略冠词。
d.es: Omite artículos con plurales generales, nombres incontables, instituciones y expresiones fijas.

d.en: Choose demonstratives by number, distance, discourse, and attitude.
d.zh: 根据单复数、距离、语篇和态度选择指示词。
d.es: Elige demostrativos según número, distancia, discurso y actitud.

d.en: Use some, any, and no across statements, questions, offers, and negatives.
d.zh: 在陈述、疑问、提议和否定中使用 some、any 和 no。
d.es: Usa some, any y no en afirmaciones, preguntas, ofertas y negativas.

d.en: Choose quantifiers according to countability and positive or negative meaning.
d.zh: 根据可数性和积极或消极含义选择数量词。
d.es: Elige cuantificadores según contabilidad y sentido positivo o negativo.

s.es: Distribuye la atención dentro de un grupo

d.en: Distinguish individual, total, two-choice, and negative distribution.
d.zh: 区分个体、整体、二选一和否定分配。
d.es: Distingue distribución individual, total, entre dos y negativa.

d.en: Edit article and determiner choices in stories, descriptions, and instructions.
d.zh: 在故事、描述和说明中修改冠词与限定词选择。
d.es: Corrige artículos y determinantes en relatos, descripciones e instrucciones.

d.en: Connect equal words, phrases, and clauses with coordinating conjunctions.
d.zh: 用并列连词连接同等的词、短语和分句。
d.es: Conecta palabras, frases y cláusulas equivalentes con conjunciones coordinantes.

d.en: Choose causal connectors according to focus, position, and formality.
d.zh: 根据焦点、位置和正式程度选择原因连词。
d.es: Elige conectores causales según foco, posición y formalidad.

name.es: So, therefore y as a result

d.en: Distinguish conjunctions from sentence adverbials when expressing results.
d.zh: 表达结果时区分连词和句子连接副词。
d.es: Distingue conjunciones y adverbiales oracionales al expresar resultados.

d.en: Choose clause-based and noun-based contrast structures accurately.
d.zh: 准确选择接从句或接名词的让步结构。
d.es: Elige correctamente estructuras de contraste con cláusula o sustantivo.

d.en: Express positive, negative, and irrelevant conditions.
d.zh: 表达肯定条件、否定条件和结果不受影响的条件。
d.es: Expresa condiciones positivas, negativas e irrelevantes.

d.en: Connect events by sequence, overlap, starting point, and endpoint.
d.zh: 通过先后、重叠、起点和终点连接事件。
d.es: Conecta eventos por secuencia, simultaneidad, inicio y final.

d.en: Maintain parallel structure with paired conjunctions.
d.zh: 使用成对连词时保持平行结构。
d.es: Mantén estructura paralela con conjunciones correlativas.

d.en: Build and edit coherent paragraphs using varied connectors.
d.zh: 使用多样连接词构建并修改连贯段落。
d.es: Construye y corrige párrafos coherentes con conectores variados.

d.en: Use gerunds as subjects, objects, and complements after common verbs and prepositions.
d.zh: 用动名词作主语、宾语及常见动词和介词后的补语。
d.es: Usa gerundios como sujeto, objeto y complemento tras verbos y preposiciones.

d.en: Use infinitives after common verbs, adjectives, nouns, and for purpose.
d.zh: 在常见动词、形容词、名词后及表示目的时使用不定式。
d.es: Usa infinitivos tras verbos, adjetivos, sustantivos y para expresar propósito.

s.en: The governing word controls the form
s.es: La palabra rectora controla la forma

d.en: Choose gerund or infinitive after verbs with one preferred pattern.
d.zh: 在只允许一种常见搭配的动词后选择动名词或不定式。
d.es: Elige gerundio o infinitivo tras verbos con un patrón preferido.

s.en: Remember doing vs remember to do
s.zh: remember doing 与 remember to do
s.es: Remember doing frente a remember to do

d.en: Distinguish patterns where gerund and infinitive produce different meanings.
d.zh: 区分动名词和不定式会产生不同意义的搭配。
d.es: Distingue patrones donde gerundio e infinitivo cambian el significado.

name.zh: 动词 + 宾语 + 不定式

d.en: Use object-infinitive patterns after request, expectation, permission, and causation verbs.
d.zh: 在请求、期望、许可和使役动词后使用宾语不定式结构。
d.es: Usa objeto más infinitivo tras verbos de petición, expectativa, permiso y causa.

d.en: Distinguish causative meanings and their required verb forms.
d.zh: 区分使役意义及其所要求的动词形式。
d.es: Distingue significados causativos y las formas verbales requeridas.

s.es: Ver que ocurre o que está ocurriendo

d.en: Choose bare infinitive or -ing after perception verbs based on viewpoint.
d.zh: 根据观察视角，在感官动词后选择原形或 -ing。
d.es: Elige infinitivo sin to o -ing tras verbos de percepción según el punto de vista.

d.en: Apply verb patterns in mixed conversations, instructions, and editing tasks.
d.zh: 在混合对话、说明和改错任务中运用动词搭配。
d.es: Aplica patrones verbales en conversaciones, instrucciones y correcciones mixtas.

d.en: Identify and build complete core English sentence patterns.
d.zh: 识别并构建完整的英语核心句型。
d.es: Identifica y construye patrones básicos completos del inglés.

d.en: Make verbs agree with simple, compound, collective, and interrupted subjects.
d.zh: 让动词与简单、并列、集体及被插入成分隔开的主语保持一致。
d.es: Haz concordar verbos con sujetos simples, compuestos, colectivos e interrumpidos.

s.en: Place information where English expects it
s.es: Coloca la información donde la espera el inglés

d.en: Control adverb, object, complement, and modifier position.
d.zh: 掌握副词、宾语、补语和修饰语的位置。
d.es: Controla la posición de adverbios, objetos, complementos y modificadores.

s.en: Invert, add an auxiliary, or keep order
s.zh: 倒装、添加助动词或保持语序
s.es: Invierte, añade auxiliar o conserva el orden

d.en: Build yes/no, wh-, subject, and embedded questions.
d.zh: 构成一般疑问句、特殊疑问句、主语疑问句和嵌入式疑问句。
d.es: Construye preguntas de sí/no, wh, de sujeto e indirectas.

s.en: Place not and choose the right auxiliary
s.zh: 放置 not 并选择正确助动词
s.es: Coloca not y elige el auxiliar correcto

d.en: Form negatives across lexical verbs, be, auxiliaries, and infinitives.
d.zh: 在实义动词、be、助动词和不定式中构成否定。
d.es: Forma negativas con verbos léxicos, be, auxiliares e infinitivos.

s.zh: 判断一个分句在哪里结束

d.en: Identify independent and dependent clauses and connect them correctly.
d.zh: 识别独立分句和从属分句并正确连接。
d.es: Identifica cláusulas independientes y dependientes y conéctalas correctamente.

name.zh: 残句、粘连句与逗号拼接
name.es: Fragmentos, oraciones fusionadas y comas indebidas

d.en: Diagnose and repair incomplete or improperly joined sentences.
d.zh: 诊断并修复不完整或连接不当的句子。
d.es: Diagnostica y corrige oraciones incompletas o mal unidas.

s.zh: 在复杂情境中保持清晰

d.en: Edit mixed sentence-level errors while preserving intended meaning.
d.zh: 在保持原意的同时修改混合句法错误。
d.es: Corrige errores sintácticos mixtos sin cambiar el significado.

name.en: Emphasis with Do and Word Choice
name.es: Énfasis con do y elección léxica

d.en: Use emphatic do, intensifiers, and focused word placement appropriately.
d.zh: 恰当地使用强调助动词 do、程度词和焦点位置。
d.es: Usa do enfático, intensificadores y colocación focal de forma adecuada.

s.es: El énfasis formal cambia el orden

d.en: Use inversion after negative and restrictive fronted expressions.
d.zh: 在前置否定和限制性表达后使用倒装。
d.es: Usa inversión tras expresiones negativas y restrictivas antepuestas.

s.en: Focus one part of the message

d.en: Use it-clefts and wh-clefts to control information focus.
d.zh: 用 it-cleft 和 wh-cleft 控制信息焦点。
d.es: Usa hendidas con it y wh para controlar el foco informativo.

d.en: Reduce relative and adverb clauses when subjects and meanings allow it.
d.zh: 在主语和意义允许时简化定语从句和状语从句。
d.es: Reduce relativas y adverbiales cuando sujeto y significado lo permiten.

d.en: Use nominalisation selectively in academic and professional writing.
d.zh: 在学术和职业写作中有选择地使用名词化。
d.es: Usa nominalización de forma selectiva en escritura académica y profesional.

s.en: Match grammar to relationship and setting
s.zh: 让语法匹配关系与场合
s.es: Ajusta la gramática a la relación y al contexto

d.en: Shift between casual, neutral, polite, and formal grammar choices.
d.zh: 在随意、中性、礼貌和正式语法选择之间切换。
d.es: Cambia entre opciones gramaticales informales, neutras, corteses y formales.

s.zh: 母语者常整体使用的结构
s.es: Patrones que los nativos usan como unidad

d.en: Recognize and use fixed grammatical frames and common collocational structures.
d.zh: 识别并使用固定语法框架和常见搭配结构。
d.es: Reconoce y usa marcos gramaticales fijos y estructuras colocacionales comunes.

s.zh: 选择准确度、语气与节奏

d.en: Edit and produce advanced structures in essays, presentations, and professional communication.
d.zh: 在文章、演示和职业沟通中修改并产出高级结构。
d.es: Corrige y produce estructuras avanzadas en ensayos, presentaciones y comunicación profesional.
